subject: Anthropology
title: Death and Dying
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Death and Dying.

hook.text: In Bali, death is a celebration. In the West, it's hidden in hospitals. Culture decides how we meet our end.

buildup.text: Every culture creates rituals around death — mourning practices, burial customs, and beliefs about what comes after.

discovery.text: Death rituals serve the living. They manage grief, reinforce social bonds, and affirm the community's worldview.

twist.text: Modern biomedicine has medicalized death, turning it from a social event into a technical failure to be managed in ICUs.

climax.text: How a culture handles death reveals its deepest values — about the self, the community, and what makes life meaningful.

punchline.text: How we die says everything about how we live.

quiz.question: What do death rituals primarily serve?
options: The living — managing grief and reinforcing social bonds
